Japanese: ９月１３日　午後　３時　２分 成歩堂法律事務所
English: October 13, 3:02 PM Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: 倉院のツボは見つかったけど‥‥ つかまっちゃったね、優作さん。
English: You know, I'm glad we found the urn and all, but poor Mr. DeLite got arrested again.

Japanese: 殺人事件があったとき、優作くん、 現場の社長室にいたんだよな‥‥。
English: Well, supposedly, Mr. DeLite was in the CEO's office when the murder occurred.

Japanese: そんなの！　きっと、何かの まちがいだよ！
English: No way, Jose! I don't buy it!

Japanese: でも‥‥それを チカラいっぱい立証したのは、
English: But the one who proved that Mr. DeLite was there...

Japanese: なるほどくん自身でしたけど。 ‥‥お席で見ていた感じでは。
English: It was Mr. Nick himself... ...At least, from what I can understand.

Japanese: まったく、ヨケイなコトしてくれた モンだよねえ、なるほどくんも。
English: Looks like you did too good of a job this time, Nick.

Japanese: ま、まあ、とにかくＫＢ警備の 殺人事件を調べてみようか。
English: Um, ah... well, how about we get started looking into the KB Security murder?

Japanese: あ。わたくしは一度、 里のほうへ戻りますね。
English: I think I'm going to head back to Kurain Village for a little while, if that's alright.

Japanese: あれ。なんで？
English: Sure. But why?

Japanese: 倉院のツボを持ち帰って、 大おばさまに見てもらってきます！
English: I'm going to bring the Sacred Urn back and have some people take a look at it!

Japanese: あ、なるほど！ じゃあ、あたしもいっしょに‥‥
English: Oh, that's a good idea! I think I'll go with--

Japanese: いえッ！　真宵さまはいいのです！
English: No, Mystic Maya! You should stay here!

Japanese: ふたりっきりで、ムードたっぷりに お出かけくださいな。
English: I want you two to spend some special quality time together, full of love and happiness!

Japanese: （むーど、って‥‥ サツジン事件の調査なんだけど）
English: (...Maybe Pearls is so caught up in her fantasy she forgot there's a murder to solve.)

Japanese: 春美ちゃんに、証拠品 《倉院のツボ》をあずけた。
English: Sacred Urn given to Pearls.

Japanese: では。おふたりとも、 ケンカしちゃダメですよ！
English: Now remember! No fighting, OK?

Japanese: ‥‥行っちまった。
English: ...She's gone.

Japanese: よおし！　じゃ、なるほどくん。 調べまくるよ、殺人事件！
English: OK, Nick! Time to get going on this murder investigation!

Japanese: 観葉植物のチャーリーくん。
English: Charley. A quite decorative plant.

Japanese: 生前、千尋さんがかわいがっていた 事務所のマスコット。
English: When she was alive, Mia really loved it. She even went so far as to make it our mascot.

Japanese: どんなにいそがしくても、 こいつの世話だけは忘れない。
English: No matter how busy I get, I never forget to water this little fella.

Japanese: ムズカシイ法律の本が あざ笑うように並んでいる。
English: Difficult-looking legal books stand in a formidable row. They mock me.

Japanese: ぜんぶ、千尋さんが 残していったモノだ。
English: These were all Mia's...

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば、自分で買った本、 ひとつもないな。
English: Wow, I just realized I didn't buy a single one of these.

Japanese: 窓の向かいがわには、 巨大なビルがそびえている。
English: There is a giant building just outside the window.

Japanese: 《ホテル・バンドー》 超ゴウカな超一流ホテルだ。
English: It's the Gatewater Hotel, a high-class, luxury hotel.

Japanese: なんでも、となり街の一角を ゴッソリ買収して、
English: The chain is getting so rich that they bought a whole chunk of the next town over...

Japanese: 超レジャー施設《バンドーランド》 の建設が始まったらしい。
English: ...and started building a huge theme park. It's going to be called "Gatewater Land".

Japanese: ‥‥２年前は小さな ビジネスホテルだったのに。
English: ...And to think that two years ago, it was just a little hotel for businessmen.

Japanese: ぼくのデスクだ。
English: It's my desk.

Japanese: あまり座る機会がないため、 いつもはホコリっぽいんだけど‥‥
English: I don't get to use it much, so the dust bunnies are starting to mount their assault.

Japanese: 今日は、まぶしいほどピカピカだ。 ‥‥きっと、春美ちゃんだな。
English: Hm? Today it's sparkly clean. I guess Pearls must've wiped them out for me.

Japanese: 古い映画のポスターだ。
English: An old movie poster.

Japanese: ムカシ千尋さんが見て、 初めて泣いた映画なのだそうだ。
English: Apparently, this was the first movie that made Mia cry when she saw it, a long time ago.

Japanese: 最近、妹の真宵ちゃんが見て、 一晩中泣いていたらしい。
English: Maya watched it recently, and said she cried all night, too!

Japanese: ‥‥ということで、最近は このポスターを張ってある。
English: ...Which, I guess, is why it's back up on the wall.

Japanese: 今度、ぼくも見てみるか。
English: I'll have to check it out one of these days.

Japanese: どうしたらいいと思う？ これから。
English: So, what do we do now?

Japanese: 決まってるじゃない！ 調べるんだよ！　殺人事件。
English: Isn't it obvious!? We should get out there and investigate the murder!

Japanese: とりあえず、ＫＢ警備会社の 場所がわからないとね。
English: Well first, we need to find out exactly where KB Security is located.

Japanese: あ。まれかさんに聞けば いいんじゃないかな。
English: ...Hey! Why don't we ask Ms. DeLite! She should know!

Japanese: あたしも、オートバイのコト もっと聞きたいし。
English: Besides, I want to ask her some stuff about motorcycles!

Japanese: バイク‥‥？ もしかして、乗りたいの？
English: Motorcycles...? You're not thinking of getting one, are you?

Japanese: いつまでも自転車でマンゾクしてる 真宵ちゃんじゃないからね。
English: I'm not the same little Maya who used to be happy with her dinky little bike, Nicky boy.

Japanese: やれやれ‥‥
English: *sigh*...

Japanese: （とりあえず、優作くんの 話も聞いておかないとな‥‥）
English: Speaking of asking around, I've got a few questions of my own for Mr. DeLite...

Japanese: じゃ、留置所にも行ってみようよ！
English: OK, well, let's make sure to go to the detention center, too!

Japanese: よかったね。 ツボが戻ってきて。
English: You must be relieved we got the Sacred Urn back, huh?

Japanese: うん！　‥‥とはいえ。 ちょっと、気になるんだよねー。
English: You bet! ...But there's something a little different about it.

Japanese: “？”じゃないよ、なるほどくん。 あからさまにヘンじゃない。
English: Don't "...?" at me! It's pretty obvious, isn't it?

Japanese: ホラ。このポスターじゃあ‥‥、 ツボの文字は“子供”なのに。
English: I mean, take a look at this. It clearly says, "I AM" on the urn in the poster.

Japanese: いつのまにか“供子” に戻ってるんだもん。
English: But the urn we got back says, "AMI", like it always used to.

Japanese: まあ‥‥たしかに。
English: Oh yeah... You're right.

Japanese: それにさ。見つかったツボ‥‥ ピンクのはん点がついてたよね。
English: Plus, the vase has pink splotches on it now...

Japanese: ‥‥あんなの、前はなかったよ。
English: ...I'm sure they weren't there before!

Japanese: （‥‥真宵ちゃんは知らないけど、 今から、１年前‥‥）
English: (Maya doesn't know, but 1 year ago... when the urn was broken...)

Japanese: （ツボは割れて、“供子”の文字は “子供”になってしまったんだ）
English: (...the repairer accidentally turned Mystic Ami's name into "I AM".)

Japanese: （そのハンニンは‥‥手先が 不器用な、春美ちゃんだった）
English: (And that repairer was one mechanically unskilled little Pearls.)
(But still...)

Japanese: （あのときは、こんなピンクの はん点は、たしかになかった）
English: (I don't remember ever seeing pink splotches on it.)

Japanese: もしかして‥‥ ニセモノなのかな、あのツボ。
English: Is it possible that urn is a fake?

Japanese: まあ‥‥それは春美ちゃんが、 倉院の里で調べてくれるから。
English: I'm sure Pearls'll find out about that once she gets back to Kurain Village.

Japanese: うん‥‥
English: Yeah, I suppose...

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥）
English: (Now that I think about it...)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん、ここしばらく 倉院の里に帰ってないな‥‥）
English: (Maya hasn't been back to Kurain Village in a long time...)

Japanese: ‥‥倉院の里では、あいかわらず みんな、修行してるの？
English: So... I guess people still go to Kurain Village to do their training, right?

Japanese: うん。一人前になるには、 キビしい修行が必要だからね。
English: Yup. If you want to become a spirit medium, you need to undergo severe training.

Japanese: でも‥‥最近、真宵ちゃんは 修行してないね。
English: So why haven't you been training lately, Maya?
Well...

Japanese: 今度、キチンと霊行道場へ行って、 みっちりやろうかな、って。
English: Lately I've been thinking of heading to a channeling dojo to do just that.

Japanese: “どうじょう”‥‥？ なんか、本格的だね。
English: A "channeling dojo", huh...? Sounds pretty serious, whatever that is.

Japanese: うん。中途ハンパな修行じゃ、 悲しい事件が起こるだけだから。
English: If you're going to train, you have to be serious. Otherwise real tragedies can happen.

Japanese: もしかして‥‥まだ、 去年のコトを‥‥？
English: Is what happened last year still bothering you...?

Japanese: （去年、里で起こった殺人事件は ‥‥霊媒を悪用したものだった）
English: (That murder in her village... It happened because the power of channeling was misused.)

Japanese: 倉院流の霊媒術で死者を呼ぶと‥‥ 霊媒師の意識は、とぎれちゃうの。
English: When a medium uses the Kurain Technique, she temporarily loses her own will.

Japanese: だから、ヘタに思念が強い霊を 呼んでしまうと‥‥
English: So when an especially strong spirit is summoned...

Japanese: 霊媒師は身体を乗っ取られて、 トンでもない事件を引き起こす。
English: ...the spirit medium can get taken over and even forced to commit terrible crimes.

Japanese: しかも‥‥霊媒師自身は、 そのときのことはおぼえていない。
English: What's worse, in those cases, the spirit medium has no memory of what happened.

Japanese: バァァァン！
English: *BANG*

Japanese: あの事件‥‥真宵ちゃんの せいじゃなかったんだよ。
English: That murder... it wasn't your fault, Maya. You know that, don't you?

Japanese: そうかもしれないけど‥‥
English: I suppose not, but...

Japanese: ‥‥なんかね、 コワくなっちゃってさ。
English: ...I guess I'm still a bit shaken up. That's all.

Japanese: （タイヘンみたいだな。 霊媒師の家元ってのも‥‥）
English: (It sounds like being the Master of Kurain is going to be a heavy responsibility...)

Japanese: 写真うつり、よくないね。
English: You're not very photogenic, are you?

Japanese: （あんまりヒトに見せないほうが いいのかな、この写真‥‥）
English: (Maybe I shouldn't show this picture to too many people...)

Japanese: （いや。でも、ぼくはこのカオを ぶら下げて歩いてるワケだし）
English: (And yet... I've walked around my whole life, showing this face of mine to the world...)

Japanese: （日々、このみっともないカオで 生き恥をさらしているのか‥‥）
English: (I wonder if I've just been making a fool of myself with this silly face...)

Japanese: ‥‥あの。カルい冗談なんだから、 あんまり悩まないでほしいな。
English: Um, I was only joking, Nick... Don't make such a sad face. You're making me feel guilty.

Japanese: 千尋さんがいなかったら、 今のぼくはなかったよ。
English: If it wasn't for Mia, I wouldn't be the person I am today.

Japanese: ホント、たよりなかったもんねー。 ムカシのなるほどくん。
English: Yeah, she told me you used to really be useless.

Japanese: あまりにたよりなかったから、 今、スゴくたのもしく見えるもん。
English: Compared to how unreliable you were then, you actually seem responsible now.

Japanese: ‥‥じつは、そんなに 変わってないのに。
English: ...Actually, you haven't changed that much, I guess.

Japanese: （ホメられた‥‥ワケじゃ なさそうだな）
English: (Maya praised me for a change! Hey, wait a second...)

Japanese: ‥‥あたしも、もっと シッカリしないとね。
English: I'm going to have to really buckle down and get serious.

Japanese: 倉院流霊媒道・家元の ムスメだもんな。
English: Well, you're the future Master of the Kurain Channeling Technique, after all.

Japanese: そうだよ。‥‥それに、 お姉さんがわりだからね。
English: Yeah... That, and I have to play the part of big sister, too.

Japanese: はみちゃんと、なるほどくんの。
English: For Pearly... and you, Nick!

Japanese: ぼ、ぼくもかよ。
English: Wha-?? Do I look like I was born yesterday!?

Japanese: いい子でしょ、はみちゃん。
English: Pearly sure is a good kid.

Japanese: うん。‥‥ちょっぴり、 思いこみがハゲしいけどね。
English: Yeah. But she tends to let her imagination get the best of her, though.

Japanese: そこがいいんだって。
English: That's one of the things I like about her, silly!

Japanese: なるほどくんにビンタする はみちゃん、シビれるもんねー。
English: Slapping you the way she did! She's a tough one alright!

Japanese: まあ‥‥たしかに シビれるけど。ほっぺが。
English: You're telling me! I think she knocked a few teeth loose.

Japanese: （でも、まだ９才なのに、 がんばってるよな、春美ちゃん）
English: (Pearls is only 9, but it's amazing how emotionally strong she is.)

Japanese: （“家族”は‥‥もう、イトコの 真宵ちゃんだけ、だもんな‥‥）
English: (After all... the only "family" she has left is her only cousin, Maya.)

Japanese: また出てきたね、イトノコさん。
English: Detective Gumshoe's on this case too, huh.

Japanese: 事件といえば、あの刑事だよ。 ‥‥グーゼンとは思えないな。
English: He seems to show up during all of our cases. It can't be a coincidence.

Japanese: ウンメイの赤いイトで しばりつけられてるんじゃない？
English: Maybe it's a sign that our fates are destined to be woven together for all time!

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっとイヤだな、それ。
English: Yeah, that'd be a real Beauty and the Beast kind of fairy tale, wouldn't it.

Japanese: ケッキョク‥‥仮面マスクは アイガ探偵だったのかな？
English: So Detective Atmey was Mask☆DeMasque after all, huh?

Japanese: そうじゃないか？ 本人も認めていたし。
English: I guess so... After all, he did admit it.

Japanese: でも‥‥優作さんだけは 認めてないよね。
English: Yes, but Mr. DeLite still hasn't changed his tune.

Japanese: あいかわらず、 自分が怪人だー、って。
English: He's still claiming that he's the real Mask☆DeMasque.

Japanese: まさか‥‥サツジンヨーギを 逃れるため‥‥じゃないよね？
English: You don't think he's just saying that to avoid the murder charge, do you?

Japanese: （まさか、な‥‥）
English: (I-I'm not sure...)

Japanese: まさか‥‥あの探偵さんが 怪人だったなんて！
English: I can hardly believe that guy was really the thief in disguise!

Japanese: あたし、ヨワいんだよねー。 そういう、ドンデン返し。
English: I never saw that coming! Then again, I stink at spotting those "plot twist" things.

Japanese: 哀牙探偵もマンゾクなんじゃ ないか。楽しんでもらえて。
English: Detective Atmey seemed to really enjoy himself today. Maybe a little too much.

Japanese: うん。今日の裁判は楽しかったな。 ‥‥まんがみたいで。
English: Ha ha, yeah. Today was pretty wild. Like the plot of some crazy comic book!

Japanese: ぼくは必死だったけどね。 ‥‥いつもどおり。
English: Great, glad you had fun while I was getting abused by the prosecutor... as always.

Japanese: カッコイイよねー、まれかさん。 バイクでツッ走るんだよ。
English: That Desirée sure is cool! I love that whole motorcycle thing!

Japanese: ‥‥それも、死のセトギワで。 あたしもやってみようかなー。
English: The way she laughs in the face of death... I want to try it, too!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん、自転車しか 乗れないだろ？
English: I thought you only knew how to ride a bike...

Japanese: でも、でも！　電動自転車 だからね、いちおう。
English: Hey! Don't forget, Nick, that it's an electric bike!

Japanese: ‥‥若いんだから、 ちゃんと運動しろよ！
English: ...E-Electric? So young and already so lazy. Whatever happened to good old exercise?

Japanese: ヤッパリさん。なるほどくんの 大親友なんだよね。
English: Oh, it's Larry. He's your old childhood friend, right?

Japanese: 小学校のころからの つきあいだからね。
English: Yeah, I've known him since elementary school.

Japanese: ひゃあ‥‥あたしとはみちゃんの つきあいより長いのかあ。
English: Wow...! That's even longer than I've known Pearly.

Japanese: きっと、楽しい思い出が いっぱいだよね！
English: You two must have lots of good memories together!

Japanese: まあね。
English: I guess so.

Japanese: ヒドい思い出に押しつぶされて、 よくおぼえてないけど。
English: But I can hardly remember them... They've been erased by all the traumatic ones.

Japanese: 今回の被害者だよね。 ムスジマさん。
English: He's the victim in this case. "Lame Blowhard"...

Japanese: だから、“ブスジマ”だよ！
English: No, it's "Kane Bullard"!

Japanese: だって、ワルそうなカオでしょ！ “ムスッ”としていてさ。
English: I know, but he looks so ugly!

Japanese: “ブスッ”としている、と 考えればいいんじゃないかな。
English: Well, why don't you remember at least the "Bull" part of his name and riff off of that?

Japanese: ‥‥あ。そりゃそうだね。 だから“ブスジマさん”かあ。
English: ...Now that you mention it, I guess he does kinda look like an ugly old bull.

Japanese: （‥‥とにかく、今回は 被害者の情報が少ないな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... We still don't know very much about this victim.)

Japanese: 優作さんに脅迫状を 送りつけていたんだよね。
English: So, he's the one who sent the blackmail letter to Mr. DeLite, right?

Japanese: そうみたいだね。 （もう少し、調べたほうがいいな）
English: It looks that way. (But we still need to look into this guy some more.)

Japanese: ね、なるほどくん。‥‥なあに、 この、カオにつけた機械は？
English: Say, Nick... What do you think that thing attached to his face is anyway?

Japanese: ぼくに聞くなよ。
English: Don't ask me.

Japanese: えー。だって、知り合い なんでしょ？　あの検事さん。
English: Why not? You know him, don't you?

Japanese: いやいや！　あんなスゴいヤツ、 見たこともないぞ。
English: I've never met that guy before in my life! And believe me, he's not exactly forgettable.

Japanese: でもさ。今日の法廷で、 あの検事さん‥‥
English: Yeah, but at the trial today, he said...

Japanese: やっと会えたな。
English: So we finally meet...

Japanese: ‥‥まるほどう　りゅういち。
English: Mr. Phoenix Trite!

Japanese: オレは、アンタと 戦わなけりゃならねえ。
English: I've returned from the depths of Hell...

Japanese: そのために‥‥ 地獄から戻ってきたのさ。
English: To do battle with you.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥向こうは ぼくを知ってるみたいだったな。
English: You're right about one thing. He sure acts like he knows me.

Japanese: あのさ、なるほどくん。
English: Hey, Nick? I was just thinking...

Japanese: 知らないウチに恨みを買うクセ、 なおしたほうがいいと思うな。
English: Maybe you should try to stop making people you've never met before into your enemies.

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくもなおしたいよ、 そんなクセ。
English: Trust me, I'd like to stop doing that, too. My poor body can only take so much.

Japanese: （アイツが、いったい ダレなのか‥‥）
English: (Who is that guy anyway? And what's his story?)

Japanese: （いつか、わかるときが 来るのかなあ‥‥）
English: (I wonder if I'll ever find out...)

Japanese: かなりカンシャしてるよね、 なるほどくんに。
English: She certainly seems grateful to you, Nick.

Japanese: タイヘンだったからね。 あの事件は。
English: Well, she was in a pretty tight spot during that terrible ordeal.

Japanese: ぼくも彼女には カンシャしてるよ。
English: Actually, I'm grateful to her, too.

Japanese: うう‥‥なんか ズルいなあ、なるほどくん。
English: Hmmm... It sounds like you're not telling me something.

Japanese: 今度、こっそり見てみようかな。 その事件の記録‥‥。
English: I'm going to take a good look at the case file next chance I get...

Japanese: まだ持ってたんだ、そのバッジ。
English: So you've still got that badge, I see.

Japanese: そ、そりゃそうだろう。 弁護士なんだから。
English: Huh!? Well, I'm a lawyer, aren't I?

Japanese: いやー、こんなに長く つづくとは思わなかったよ。
English: Yeah, but I guess I just didn't think you'd keep on being one for this long.

Japanese: あぶなっかしいからねー、 なるほどくんのベンゴ。
English: You have to admit, you've had some close brushes with death because of your job.

Japanese: （それは、真宵ちゃんの霊媒も 似たようなモノだと思うけど）
English: (...I fail to see how being a lawyer is be more dangerous than channeling dead people.)

Japanese: たしかに、 倉院のツボは見つかったけど‥‥
English: I'm glad we got the Sacred Urn back and all...

Japanese: なんか、スッキリしない カオしてるね。
English: So then how come you don't look any happier?

Japanese: するワケないでしょ！
English: Why should I!?

Japanese: 書いてある文字は《供子》か 《子供》かハッキリしないし、
English: I don't know if it should say "AMI" or "I AM" on it anymore...

Japanese: なんか、オシャレなピンクの 水玉もようも増えてるし！
English: ...and now it's got big pink polka dots on it that weren't there before!

Japanese: 気にするほどのコトじゃ ないんじゃないか？
English: Don't you think you're overreacting just a little?

Japanese: あー、言ったね、なるほどくん！ じゃあ、じゃあ‥‥そうだなあ。
English: Overreacting!? Nick! I can't believe you said that! I mean, it's like... like... I got it!

Japanese: あたしがピンクの水玉もようの 装束をイキに着こんで、
English: It's like if I came in wearing polka dots on my acolyte costume every day and said...

Japanese: 『あたし、イヨマちゃん！』 なんて言いだしたら、どうよ！
English: "Hi, Nick! I'm Ayam!" Well!? Do you still think it's no big deal?

Japanese: ‥‥それは‥‥なんというか、 一大事だな。
English: ...I guess... it would be... um... a real disaster...?

Japanese: でしょ！　でしょ！ ‥‥そういうコトなんだよ！
English: You see! You see! Well that's how I feel!

Japanese: あたしも知らなかったよ、 こんなものがあったなんて。
English: You know, even I didn't know this statue existed until a little while ago.

Japanese: “倉院流・分家の霊場から 借りてきた”って言ってたな。
English: Didn't Adrian say it's from a Fey branch family's training hall or something?

Japanese: 全国に、たくさんあるんだよ。 倉院流の修行場。
English: Yes, there are actually a lot of training halls scattered all over the country.

Japanese: さみしい山奥に、ひっそりと。 いくつあるか知らないけど。
English: Hidden away deep in desolate mountains...

Japanese: （‥‥さみしくなくちゃ ダメなのかな、修行場‥‥）
English: (Why does it always have to be "desolate" mountains...?)

Japanese: これ‥‥ホンモノの 仮面マスクが作ったんだよな。
English: So this was made by the real Mask☆DeMasque, huh?

Japanese: イトノコ刑事がタイコ判を 押してたからね。
English: Yup, Detective Gumshoe said it was the real thing.

Japanese: “このエンブレムこそ、 ホンモノの証拠ッス”って。
English: "One look at this mark and you know it's the real deal, pal."

Japanese: あの刑事のタイコ判は、 三文判よりチカラ不足だけどね。
English: Yeah, but you do realize whose deductive reasoning skills we're talking about, right...?

Japanese: あいかわらず曲がってるね。 くにゃっ、て。
English: It's still bent, isn't it?

Japanese: アイガ探偵、これで後ろアタマを 殴られた、って言ってたけど‥‥
English: Detective Atmey said it got bent when the thief hit him on the head with it.

Japanese: ウソだったんだろうね。 彼自身が怪人だったんだから。
English: Well, it looks like he ended up the bent one after he turned out to be the thief.

Japanese: ワザと曲げて、凶器であるように 見せかけたんだ。
English: He must have damaged the sword himself to back up his phony story.

Japanese: ううん‥‥さすが頭脳派だなあ、 アイガ探偵め‥‥。
English: Yeah... I guess he really does have a brawny mind, huh?

Japanese: このデータによると、 怪人があらわれた時間は‥‥
English: According to this data, the thief showed up at the basement warehouse at...

Japanese: 夜中の１時‥‥なんだよね。
English: Um, 1 o'clock in the morning, right?

Japanese: でも、そのデータは哀牙探偵が 用意したものだからなあ。
English: Yeah, but remember, this data came from Atmey...

Japanese: 好きに細工できたはずだよ。
English: For all we know, he might have somehow tampered with it.

Japanese: でも、でも。こっちの写真にも 記録されてるよ、時間。
English: But... But then, what about this photo? It's got the time on it, too!

Japanese: （‥‥この防犯カメラは、 高菱屋側が用意したものだ）
English: (Yeah, and the security camera belonged to Lordly Tailor.)

Japanese: （哀牙探偵に、この時間表示を 細工することは、できない‥‥）
English: (Atmey couldn't have tampered with the photo's timestamp to show the wrong time...)

Japanese: じゃあ、やっぱり ホンモノなんだよ、この記録は。
English: I guess that means this data is accurate after all.

Japanese: この写真に写っている 仮面マスク‥‥
English: So the Mask☆DeMasque in this photo...

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり、 あのアイガ探偵なのかな。
English: ...is Detective Atmey in disguise, huh?

Japanese: そうなるね。
English: It looks that way.

Japanese: （哀牙探偵は、倉庫に防犯カメラが あるのを知っていた‥‥）
English: (Atmey knew there was a security camera set up in the basement...)

Japanese: （この写真を撮られるのも、 計画のうちだったのかな‥‥？）
English: (So I'm guessing this photo was probably a part of his elaborate plan...)

Japanese: 優作さん‥‥キョーハク されていたんだね‥‥。
English: So Mr. DeLite was being blackmailed, huh?

Japanese: 自分が、ムカシはたらいていた 会社の社長さんに。
English: By the CEO of the security company he used to work for.

Japanese: そうらしいね。 （調べてみる必要があるな‥‥）
English: Yeah, it looks like it. (Sounds like we still need to do some more poking around...)

Japanese: なるほどくんも、ダメだよ。 ワルいコトしちゃ。
English: You'd better watch yourself and not do anything naughty either, Nick.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんに キョーハクされるからな‥‥）
English: (Was that a threat? Is she trying to blackmail ME now...?)

Japanese: 事件当夜、殺人のあったビルで このサイフが発見された‥‥
English: This was found in the building where the crime took place on the very night of the murder.

Japanese: ついでに、中に入っていた キーカードの使用時刻は‥‥
English: And according to the time record on the key card inside...

Japanese: ズバリ、殺人の起こった時間。 イヤな感じだねえ‥‥。
English: Yeah, it was used right around the time of the murder... Doesn't sound very good, huh?

Japanese: それ、現場に落ちていたんだよね。
English: This was found on the floor of the basement warehouse.

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥哀牙探偵が 自分で置いたのかな。
English: So does that mean... Detective Atmey put it there himself?

Japanese: そうじゃないかな。
English: That's the only explanation I can think of.

Japanese: いかにも、現場で格闘があった ように見せかけるために、ね。
English: I guess he wanted to make sure it looked like he'd struggled with the thief that night.

Japanese: どう思う？　これ‥‥
English: What do you think about this?

Japanese: うーん‥‥事件に カンケイありそうだけど‥‥
English: Hmmm... It seems like it has something to do with this case...

Japanese: ちょっと、あたしには わからないな。
English: But I'm not really sure...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: October 13 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: だから、ぼくじゃありませーーーん
English: I already told you! It's not meeeeeeeee

Japanese: この、か細く耳をつきさす もの悲しげなヒメイは‥‥
English: A sad, pitiful whine that tapers into silence...

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、優作くんは 取り調べ中らしいね。
English: Sounds like they're interrogating Mr. DeLite right now.

Japanese: ううう‥‥時間がないのに。
English: Man, and we don't have enough time as it is.

Japanese: しかたないよ。警察も あわててるんじゃないかな。
English: Yeah, well, I guess the police are going crazy just like we are.

Japanese: きのうまで怪人だと思っていたら、 今日は殺人事件の容疑者だからね。
English: Yesterday they thought he was just a thief, but now they got a murder case on their hands.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥あそこの看守さんも、 ちょっとそわそわしてるね。
English: I guess you're right... That guard over there looks a bit on edge, too.

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、あとでもう一度 来てみようか。
English: Come on. We'll just have to come back later.

Japanese: よし！　他をあたるよ、 なるほどくん！
English: OK! Let's go check out some other place, Nick!

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: どうやら今は、 することがないのだろう。
English: It looks like he doesn't have much to do right now.

Japanese: 向かい側のカメラに向かって おもしろいカオをしている。
English: I can tell because he's looking at the camera and making funny faces.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile, you're on candid camera.

Japanese: どうやら今は、 監視するものがないのだろう。
English: The camera doesn't seem to have a lot to tape right now.

Japanese: 向かい側の看守を やさしく見守っている。
English: The only thing it's watching now is that guard over there.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: October 13 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: あ！　な、成歩堂さんッ！
English: Ah! M-Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 優作さん‥‥。 取り調べ、終わったんですか？
English: Mr. DeLite... Did they finish their interrogation?

Japanese: はい！　ボクを‥‥ボクを、 見捨てないでくださーーーい！
English: Yes, but...! P-Please don't leave me alone anymoooooore!

Japanese: ‥‥優作くん。ぼくたちに、 ウソをついていましたね？
English: ...Mr. DeLite, you lied to us before, didn't you?

Japanese: あ。‥‥そ、それは‥‥
English: W-Well... Err, you see...

Japanese: あの晩、高菱屋百貨店で 倉院のツボの盗難事件が発生した。
English: On the same night the Sacred Urn was stolen from Lordly Tailor Department Store...

Japanese: 同時に、きみは脅迫状で、 ＫＢ警備に呼び出されていた。
English: ...a blackmail letter you got summoned you to KB Security to hand over some money.

Japanese: そして、そこでは‥‥ 毒島社長が殺害されていた。
English: And then, that's where the CEO, Kane Bullard, was murdered.

Japanese: でも‥‥天杉　優作は、 ひとりしかいない。
English: But... there's only one Ron DeLite, am I right?

Japanese: ‥‥あの晩、きみは 何をしていたのか‥‥？
English: So the only question is... Where were you that night?

Japanese: 今度こそ、本当のコトを 聞かせてもらおうかな。
English: This time I want to hear the whole truth... Your life depends on it.

Japanese: は、はい‥‥。
English: O-OK...

Japanese: 優作くん‥‥。最後に１つだけ、 カクニンしておくよ。
English: Ron, before we go, there's one more thing I want to ask you.

Japanese: な、なんですか。 そんな、アタマをトガらせて‥‥
English: Y-Yes...? B-But please don't hurt me...

Japanese: ブスジマ社長を殺害したのは ‥‥本当に、きみじゃないんだね？
English: Mr. Kane Bullard... Do you swear that it wasn't you who killed him?

Japanese: ち‥‥ちがいます！　もう‥‥ ウソはつきません！
English: Y-Yes, of course! I could never...! I-I'm not lying!

Japanese: ボク、死体を金庫に かくしただけなんです。
English: All I did was hide his body in the safe!

Japanese: それがバレるのがコワくて‥‥ きのう、自首したんです。
English: But then I was afraid they'd discover what I did. So I turned myself in yesterday!

Japanese: どうして、そんなコトを？
English: Um, why?

Japanese: 怪人として有罪になれば‥‥、 アリバイができますからね‥‥。
English: Well, if the judge had ruled that I was guilty of robbery, then I'd have an alibi, right?

Japanese: ううん‥‥。 アタマいいんだ、優作さん。
English: Hmm... I guess so. You're really clever, Mr. DeLite.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥優作くんを 信用するしかないみたいだな）
English: (I guess I have no choice but to take Mr. DeLite at his word.)

Japanese: ‥‥真宵さまっ！
English: Mystic Maya!

Japanese: あ‥‥はみちゃん！
English: Hey...! Pearly!

Japanese: ただいま戻りました！
English: I'm back!

Japanese: 春美ちゃん、今まで 何してたんだっけ‥‥？
English: Hey Pearls. So what have you been up to this whole time...?

Japanese: このツボです！　倉院の里で、 調べてもらっていたのですよ！
English: The Sacred Urn, Mr. Nick! I took it back to Kurain Village to have it examined!

Japanese: で？　で？　どうだった？
English: And? And? What did you find out?

Japanese: はい！　ご心配なく。 まちがいなくホンモノだそうです！
English: Well there's no need to worry! They said it's the real urn!

Japanese: うわあ、よかったねえ！ いやホントに。
English: Whew! That's a relief! I was really worried!

Japanese: ただ‥‥ひとつだけ、問題が。
English: But there's one small problem.

Japanese: モンダイ‥‥？
English: Problem?

Japanese: この、おちゃめな ピンクの模様なのですが‥‥
English: Um, these cute little pink splotches...

Japanese: どうやら、ペンキのようで‥‥ しかも、最近ついたものなのです！
English: They said that it's paint... And that they were put on the urn recently!

Japanese: （ツボに現れたピンクの模様、か。 ‥‥ま、どうでもいいけど）
English: Why are we talking about the pink splotches again? What's the big deal?

Japanese: どうでもよくないッ！ ちゃんと調べるんだよ！
English: What's the big deal!? We've gotta find out how they got there! That's the big deal!

Japanese: ちゃんと調べるのですよ、 なるほどくん！
English: Yes, Mr. Nick! We've gotta find out how they got there!

Japanese: わ、わかったよ。 ツボのコトも、調べてみよう。
English: O-OK, OK! We'll go find out how they got there!

Japanese: あの‥‥ボクのコトも、 忘れないでくださいね‥‥。
English: P-Please don't forget about me...

Japanese: 《倉院のツボ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Sacred Urn refiled into the Court Record.

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: 怪人から殺人犯にヘンシンした 被告人を前にして、
English: He looks pretty nervous...

Japanese: かなりキンチョーぎみに見える。
English: ...standing near someone who went from thief to murderer overnight.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile, you're on candid camera.

Japanese: 優作くん。きみはまだ、 仮面マスクは自分だ、と‥‥？
English: Mr. DeLite, do you still insist that you are Mask☆DeMasque...?

Japanese: だから、きのうから そう言ってるじゃないですか。
English: Isn't that what I've been saying since yesterday?

Japanese: ‥‥アッサリ言われたね。
English: That was a quick response...

Japanese: ちょっとだけ、ハラが立つな。
English: Tell me about it. To be honest, it's starting to get irritating.

Japanese: でも、でも、優作さん！
English: B-But listen, Mr. DeLite!

Japanese: 今日の裁判で、カイジンの 正体は暴かれたじゃないですか！
English: At the trial today, we learned the true identity of the thief, didn't we!?

Japanese: あーっはっはっはっはっはっは！ はっはっはっはっはっはっ！
English: Hee hee hee ha ha ha Aha ha ha ha ha ha ha! Ho ho ho hee hee hee!

Japanese: ‥‥さあさあみなさん、 ごらんくださあい‥‥！
English: Take a good look, everyone!

Japanese: 天才にふさわしき好敵手を求めて、 ついに自ら犯罪に手を染めた‥‥
English: Unable to find a rival worthy of my genius, I was forced to create one by myself!

Japanese: あわれなピエロでござあい‥‥
English: Here I am! The tragic clown...

Japanese: たしかに‥‥あのツボを盗んだ のは、ボクじゃないですけど。
English: I guess it's true... I wasn't the one who stole that urn...

Japanese: そりゃ、そうですよね。 ＫＢ警備にいたんですから。
English: Of course not. After all, you were at KB Security at the time.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥この写真の怪人の 正体は、やっぱり‥‥
English: So then the person dressed up as Mask☆DeMasque in this photo...

Japanese: アイガ探偵‥‥ ってコトになるよね‥‥。
English: ...It's gotta be Detective Atmey.

Japanese: あの晩‥‥きみは、高菱屋ではなく ＫＢ警備に行ったんだね？
English: So that night, you didn't go to Lordly Tailor, you went to KB Security, right?

Japanese: はい。‥‥ボク、ヤクソクの時間 どおり、ＫＢ警備に行ったんです。
English: Yes... I went to KB Security at the time the blackmail note said I should.

Japanese: うんうん。‥‥それで？
English: Alright... What happened next?

Japanese: ムカシ社員だったから、 社長室の場所は知っていました。
English: Well, I used to work there, so I knew where the CEO's office was.

Japanese: ノックしたんですけど‥‥ 返事がなくて。
English: I knocked... but there was no answer.

Japanese: だから、キーカードを 使って、ロックをはずしました。
English: So then I used the key card to unlock the door.

Japanese: （サイフを落としたのは たぶん、そのときだな‥‥）
English: (That's probably when he dropped his wallet...)

Japanese: 社長室に入ったら‥‥、 だれかが立っていたんです。
English: When I went into the CEO's office... someone was in there.

Japanese: “だれか”‥‥
English: "Someone"...?

Japanese: そいつに‥‥いきなり、 アタマをガツン！　って。
English: Then suddenly they bashed me over the head! Bam!

Japanese: ブスジマ社長さんが 殴ったのかな‥‥？
English: Was it Kane Bullard that hit you...?

Japanese: わかりません。
English: I don't know.

Japanese: ボクがクラクラしている間に、 そいつ、逃げちゃったみたいで。
English: The person ran away while I was still stunned.

Japanese: 気がついたら‥‥もう、 ビックリしました。
English: When I came to my senses, the sight I saw left me speechless.

Japanese: 目の前に、社長の死体が！ ボクも死ぬかと思いましたよ！
English: The dead body of the CEO was right there in front of me! I thought I'd die myself!

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、死体をなんとか しようと思いました。
English: Anyway, I thought I should do something with the body...

Japanese: それで、あの金庫に‥‥？
English: So that's why you put it in the safe...?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。そうなんです。
English: ...Yes, that's right.

Japanese: ボク、警備チームのチーフだった から、開け方を知ってたんですよ。
English: I used to be the chief of one of the security teams, so I knew how to open it.

Japanese: それで‥‥そのあとは？ どうしたんですか？
English: OK... And what did you do after that?

Japanese: そりゃもう、とりあえず 逃げましたよ！
English: Well, I got out of there, for starters!

Japanese: あのビルの防犯カメラも、 ボクが設置しましたからね！
English: I was the one who set up the security cameras in that building...

Japanese: 自分の姿が映らないように、 うまーいコト切り抜けました！
English: ...so I knew how to avoid being spotted by them!

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Nick...

Japanese: どう考えてもハンニンぽいよ。 ‥‥優作さん。
English: All of a sudden, Mr. DeLite kinda sounds like the murderer to me...

Japanese: （それを言っちゃあ‥‥）
English: (Please don't say that...)

Japanese: キョーハクジョーって‥‥ コレが初めてじゃないんですか？
English: This blackmail letter... Is this the first one you got?

Japanese: モチロン、ちがいますよ！
English: No, of course not!

Japanese: ‥‥今回みたいに、どこかに 呼び出されたのは初めてですけど。
English: But this is the first one that ever called me out to a specific location.

Japanese: 最初の脅迫状は‥‥ この事件の、すぐあとに‥‥？
English: So, did you start receiving blackmail letters after this incident...?

Japanese: ええ。《エマノンの涙》事件から 数日たったころでしたね。
English: Yes. Just a few days after the "Tear of Emanon" heist...

Japanese: ウチに脅迫状が届いたんです。 《おまえがやったんだろう》って。
English: That first letter... It said, "I know you did it".

Japanese: いきなりバレちゃったんですか。
English: So someone found out about your true identity? Just like that?

Japanese: いやー、これでなかなか ムズカシイですよね、怪人も。
English: Ah, it's not easy being a master thief, you know!

Japanese: 《決定的な証拠もあるから、 カクゴしろ》なんて言われて。
English: "I've got proof that it was you, so give up!" it went on to say.

Japanese: それで、せっかくのエモノの 宝石も取りあげられちゃって‥‥
English: So in the end, I had to give up the treasure I went through all that trouble to steal...

Japanese: そうなんですか‥‥‥‥
English: Is that right...

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと！ “取りあげられた”というのは？
English: Hey! Hang on a second! What do you mean you had to "give it up"?

Japanese: あ、シンパイいりませんよ。 指定の貸金庫に宝石を預けたら、
English: Oh, don't worry. After I put the jewel in the safe-deposit box the letter specified...

Japanese: あとで１００万円も 送ってきてくれましたからね。
English: ...someone sent me $10,000!

Japanese: （だれもシンパイなんか してないって‥‥）
English: (No one said anything about me being worried, you know...)

Japanese: それからです。ボクのところに、 計画書が届くようになったのは。
English: After that, I started getting the plans in the mail.

Japanese: け、けいかくしょ‥‥？
English: P-Plans...?

Japanese: あの。“計画書”というのは‥‥？
English: What are these "plans" you're talking about?

Japanese: 宝冠とか、絵とか、美術品‥‥ 狙うエモノと、盗み出す方法。
English: They were instructions on how to steal a crown or painting or some other rare treasure.

Japanese: 警備の盲点や逃げ道、トレーニング 方法までキッチリ書かれていて。
English: They showed security blind spots, escape routes and even suggested training methods.

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ、まさか。 犯行の計画を立てたのは‥‥
English: So you mean that the one who planned the heists wasn't you...?

Japanese: ええ。２回目からは、 ボクじゃないですよ。
English: No, it wasn't. I only planned the very first one.

Japanese: だれか親切なヒトが、イカした 大作戦を考えてくれるんですよ！
English: After that, I received plans from some very kind person! Incredibly detailed plans!

Japanese: 優作さん、キョーハク者に カンシャしてるよ‥‥
English: It sounds like Mr. DeLite is thankful to the person that was blackmailing him.

Japanese: （やはり‥‥怪人☆仮面マスクは、 この優作くんだったのか‥‥？）
English: (So Ron DeLite was Mask☆DeMasque after all...?)

Japanese: （他にいるんだ。犯罪の計画を 立てた人物が、もうひとり！）
English: (Someone else is behind the thefts. Someone who planned it all out in detail!)

Japanese: 計画どおりに盗み出したエモノは、 指定の貸金庫に預けるんです。
English: All I had to do was deposit the treasures I stole into the safe-deposit box.

Japanese: そうすると、おカネが 送られてくるんですよねー。
English: Then I just waited for the cash to come in the mail.

Japanese: （‥‥そんな、うれしそうな カオで言われても‥‥）
English: (Could you try not to look so gleeful about it...?)

Japanese: じゃあ、《倉院のツボ》を 狙ったのも‥‥？
English: So you went after the Sacred Urn because of one of those plans, too?

Japanese: そんなツボ‥‥じつは、 見たこともないんですよ。
English: Well, see... truth is, I've never seen the urn.

Japanese: ボクは、計画書に書いてあるモノを 計画どおりに盗むだけですから。
English: All I did was follow the instructions and steal what I was told to steal.

Japanese: 優作くん‥‥。今の証言、 まちがいないね？
English: Mr. DeLite... Is everything you've just told me the truth?

Japanese: ええ。‥‥でも、 まれかちゃんにはナイショですよ？
English: Yes... B-But please don't tell Dessie. OK?

Japanese: 《天杉優作の証言書》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Ron's Testimony added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 優作さん。ＫＢ警備、 やめさせられたんですよね？
English: Mr. DeLite, is it true that one year ago...

Japanese: 今から、１年ぐらい前に‥‥
English: ...you were forced to quit KB Security?

Japanese: あ！　そ、それ！ ‥‥まれかちゃんには。
English: Ah! H-How did you...!?

Japanese: まれかちゃんには、 言わないでくださーーーい！
English: I-I'm begging you! Please don't tell Dessie! Pleeeease!

Japanese: い、いえ。 まだ言ってませんけど‥‥
English: D-Don't worry. We haven't told anyone yet...

Japanese: たた、助かった‥‥‥‥いや。
English: Whew! Thank goodness... Err, no... I umm...

Japanese: でも、どうせアレかな。 かくしたって、いつかはバレるしな
English: But I suppose I'll have to tell her sometime... She'll find out eventually

Japanese: どうして話さないんですか？ まれかさんに。
English: Why have you been hiding it from her anyway?

Japanese: まれかちゃん、曲がったコトが 大キライなんですよ。
English: Dessie would despise me if she ever found out I was living a life of crime.

Japanese: 犯罪者‥‥特に怪人なんて、 ユルしてくれるワケがないから‥‥
English: A criminal, a thief... She'd never forgive me! My marriage would be over!

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ‥‥なんで ワザワザ怪人なんかに？
English: Knowing that, why did you become a thief in the first place?

Japanese: まれかちゃん、それはキモチよく おカネを使うんですよ。
English: Because Dessie spends money like it's water.

Japanese: それだけのおカネが もらえるシゴト‥‥
English: There's no job in the world that could bring in enough money...

Japanese: ボク、怪人しか 思いつかなかったんです。
English: Except being a thief. At least, that's what I thought anyway.

Japanese: （まれかさんのためだったんだ ‥‥怪人☆仮面マスク‥‥）
English: (So he became Mask☆DeMasque for Desirée, huh...?)

Japanese: いやー、スゴいですよね。 弁護士さん、なんて。
English: A great lawyer and so young! You really are an inspiration!

Japanese: さぞかし、かせいで らっしゃるんでしょうねえ。
English: I bet you make lots and lots of money, don't you?

Japanese: 怪人に言われると、ちょっと イヤミに聞こえるね。
English: That doesn't sound right coming from a thief, if you know what I mean...

Japanese: スミマセン‥‥ ボク、なんてヒドいオトコなんだろ
English: I'm sorry... I suppose you could say I'm nothing but a terrible lowlife

Japanese: ど、どうかしましたか？
English: Wh-Why do you say that?

Japanese: ‥‥あの、初めてなんです。 盗難事件の被害者とお会いするの。
English: ...This is the first time I've ever had to actually face a victim of one of my crimes.

Japanese: （倉院のツボのコトか‥‥）
English: (I guess he's talking about the Sacred Urn...)

Japanese: あ。でも、ツボを盗んだのは 優作さんじゃないんですよね？
English: Yeah, but I thought that you weren't the one who stole my urn...

Japanese: た、たしかに、 ツボはそうなんですけど‥‥
English: Th-That's true about the urn, I guess...

Japanese: やっぱり、怪人なんかやめて、 ロックンローラーになればよかった
English: Maybe I should give up this life of crime and try becoming a professional cowbell player

Japanese: このヒト、怪人☆仮面マスク担当の 刑事さんですね。
English: This is the detective that was in charge of the Mask☆DeMasque investigations.

Japanese: きのう自首したら、ナミダを流して よろこんでくれました。
English: He was so happy when I surrendered yesterday that he actually shed tears of joy.

Japanese: へええ。あの、イトノコさんが？
English: What? Gumshoe cried!? Really!? Was it a single manly tear or did he let loose and sob?

Japanese: ボクもついつい、 もらい泣きしちゃって。
English: Sobbed. I started weeping myself, too. It was truly infectious!

Japanese: いやあ、カンドーしたなあ。 あのときは。
English: A truly moving moment, if I do say so myself.

Japanese: ‥‥さぞ、ソーゼツな光景 だったんでしょうねえ‥‥。
English: ...That must've been one heck of a beautiful scene.

Japanese: “もらい泣き”のイミが よくわからないけどな。
English: (If beauty is in the eye of the beholder, I'm convinced that these two are blind.)

Japanese: 怪人になろうと決めて、ボク、 いろいろ勉強したんですよ。
English: Once I decided to become a thief, I started studying like crazy.

Japanese: ‥‥怪人について？
English: Studying!? Like what? Petty Larceny 101? The History of Pilfering?

Japanese: はい。小説や映画を見て、 大センパイたちのカツヤクを。
English: Ha. No, but I watched a lot of movies and read a lot of novels about great thieves.

Japanese: へええ。ネッシンなんですねー。
English: Oh yeah? I admire you for doing your homework, if for nothing else.

Japanese: みなさん、とても はずかしいカッコウをなさって、
English: All the great ones had their own trademarks. Flashy costumes, calling cards...

Japanese: 予告状に高笑い。そして、 キメぜりふは“サラバだ諸君”。
English: ...unique laughs, and catchy slogans, like, "Farewell, my friends!"

Japanese: ‥‥このへんは、やはりキッチリ 押さえないと、って思って。
English: I figured I needed to do a good job with that if I was going to make a splash.

Japanese: ホント、怪人にならないと、 なかなか言えないよねー。
English: Yeah, I guess you really can't say goofy stuff like that unless you're a master thief.

Japanese: “サラバ”も“ショクン”も。
English: "Farewell" or "We shall meet again"... You know, hokey stuff like that.

Japanese: さすが、名探偵さんですよね。
English: He's an Ace Detective, alright.

Japanese: １回目の事件から毎回、ボクを いい感じに追いつめますからねえ。
English: He's been hot on my trail ever since the first heist.

Japanese: でも‥‥今日の法廷で、 ショーゲキの告白をしましたよね。
English: Yes, but... He made a pretty stunning confession in court today.

Japanese: “この自分こそ、仮面マスクだ！” ‥‥って。
English: "It was me all along! I am Mask☆DeMasque!"...

Japanese: ううん‥‥思いこみがハゲしいん でしょうね、たぶん。
English: I don't know what to say... I guess he's just delusional.

Japanese: （まれかさんみたいなコト 言ってるぞ‥‥）
English: (That's exactly what your wife said about you...)

Japanese: じつは‥‥ときどき、思うんです。
English: I'm ashamed to admit it...

Japanese: もしかして‥‥ボク、怪人には 向いてないんじゃないか、って。
English: ...but sometimes I wonder if I'm really cut out to be a thief.

Japanese: そりゃそうでしょう！
English: Well, isn't that obvious?

Japanese: ゼンゼン似合いませんよ！
English: It doesn't suit you at all!

Japanese: うううう‥‥。やっぱり、 気のせいじゃなかったのか‥‥
English: Uuugh... I knew it... I guess I'm not the only one who came to that conclusion.

Japanese: まれかちゃん‥‥犯罪者だけは ユルせないらしいんですよ。
English: Dessie... She really has a thing against criminals.

Japanese: その理由‥‥優作さん、 ご存じなんですか？
English: Do you have any idea why that is, Mr. DeLite?

Japanese: さあ‥‥。詳しいハナシは、 彼女に聞いてもらえますか。
English: Well... If you want to know the details, you'll have to ask her.

Japanese: とにかくボク、ＫＢ警備を クビになっちゃって‥‥
English: But the thing is, after I lost my job at KB Security...

Japanese: まれかちゃんのために、 おカネがほしかったんです。
English: I needed to earn money to keep Dessie satisfied... Lots of money.

Japanese: でも‥‥なかなかシゴトが 決まらなくて。
English: But... I just couldn't seem to find a good job.

Japanese: ケッキョク、まれかちゃんが イチバンきらいな、怪人に‥‥
English: That's why I wound up becoming the thing that Dessie hates most... A thief.

Japanese: あ、シャチョー‥‥ おキノドクです。
English: Mr. Bullard... my old boss. It's so sad...

Japanese: 何か、思い当たるコトは ありますか？
English: Do you have any idea what might have happened?

Japanese: ‥‥毒島さんが 殺害されたコトについて。
English: Was there anyone that might have wanted him dead?

Japanese: そうですね‥‥なにかと 敵の多いヒトでしたから。
English: Oh yeah, sure... Mr. Bullard was the type of guy who made lots of enemies.

Japanese: 脅迫まがいのコトをしてみたり。
English: He did lots of threatening things to people...

Japanese: いやいや。“まがい”じゃなくて、 脅迫そのものじゃないですか。
English: Wait, wait, do you mean he scared people, or that he threaten them with blackmail?

Japanese: まあ‥‥、そういうヒト でしたねえ。
English: Well, I guess they're kind of the same, right?

Japanese: カッコイイですよねー、 あこがれちゃうなー。
English: Isn't he great! I wish I could be half as cool as him!

Japanese: ボクが怪人だって信じて くれたの、このヒトだけだったし。
English: Besides, he's the only one that really believed me when I said I was the thief!

Japanese: いやいや。それは、きみを殺人罪で 再タイホするためだったんだよ。
English: Hey, hang on a second! This is the guy that had you arrested for murder!

Japanese: ‥‥そんなワルいヒトには 見えなかったけどなあ‥‥
English: Well, I still don't think that makes him a bad person...

Japanese: （どんなふうに見えたんだよ、 いったい‥‥）
English: (What in the world do you see in that coffee-crazed prosecutor!?)

Japanese: どうですか？　これなんですけど。
English: What do you think about this?

Japanese: そういうのはボク、ちょっと。 ‥‥‥‥いや。
English: Hmm... I don't really think I can help you with that. Err, no, that's not it...

Japanese: ちょっとちがうか。 “そういうの”って、どういうのか
English: It's just that the thing is I don't really know if I can say one way or the other

Japanese: よく聞こえないね。
English: I didn't really catch that.

Japanese: 聞くイミもなさそうだよ。
English: Don't worry, you didn't miss anything.

Japanese: いやー、フシギですよねー。
English: That's kind of strange...

Japanese: なにがですか？
English: What is?

Japanese: 仮面マスクのエモノ‥‥今までは 有名な美術品ばかりだったのに。
English: Up until now, Mask☆DeMasque has gone after famous works of art or treasures.

Japanese: いきなり、こんなシブくてジミな マニア向けのツボですから。
English: Why did he suddenly decide to steal this plain-looking urn that nobody would want?

Japanese: ‥‥他人ゴトみたいに 言わないでほしいな‥‥
English: You make it sound like you had nothing to do with the theft.

Japanese: だって‥‥このツボをエモノに 選んだの、ボクじゃないですから。
English: Well... It wasn't me that chose to steal this urn.

Japanese: へえ。ボク、こういうの、 けっこう好きなんですよ。
English: Wow, this is cool! I'm really into this kind of stuff.

Japanese: ホントですか！　あたしたちの お宝なんですよ！
English: Oh yeah? It's one of our most valuable treasures!

Japanese: もし無罪になったら、ゼッタイ 見に行きますから。
English: If I ever get out of here, me and Dessie will go to Lordly Tailor...

Japanese: まれかちゃんといっしょに、 真宵さんたちの秘宝展。
English: ...to check out the whole Treasures of Kurain Exhibit!

Japanese: ‥‥あの、盗まないでくださいね。 あたしたちの、お宝‥‥。
English: Um, OK, I guess... Just promise me you won't steal anything, OK?

Japanese: やだなあ。シゴト抜きで 行くに決まってるじゃないですか！
English: Oh don't worry! We'd be going when I'm not on the job as Mask☆DeMasque.

Japanese: （ビミョーな会話だな‥‥）
English: (Well, that's reassuring...)

Japanese: この予告状‥‥ 優作くんが作ったの？
English: This calling card... Did you send this?

Japanese: いえ‥‥それが。ボク、 知らないんですよねー。それ。
English: No...! Err, that is... I don't know anything about that.

Japanese: だって、あの晩は それどころじゃなかったし。
English: I mean, I had other plans for that night already...

Japanese: （脅迫状が届いていた わけだからな‥‥）
English: (That's true... He did get that blackmail letter, after all...)

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥アイガ探偵なのかな。 予告状を書いたの。
English: What about Detective Atmey...? Could he be the one that sent it?

Japanese: そうみたいだね。 他に考えられないし。
English: It looks that way. I can't think of anyone else.

Japanese: あの夜は、しかたなくＫＢ警備に 行ったんです。
English: Yeah... That night, because of the letter, I had no choice but to go to KB Security.

Japanese: これ‥‥どう見ても、ホンモノの 仮面マスクですよね。
English: This photo... This has got to be the real Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: ええ。このボクもタイコ判を 押しますよ！
English: I'm sure it is. I know Mask☆DeMasque when I see him.

Japanese: でも‥‥優作くんじゃ ないんだよね。
English: Yes, but... This isn't you, right Mr. DeLite?

Japanese: はあ、そうですねえ。 ボク、ＫＢ警備にいましたから。
English: Well, err... No... I was at KB Security then.

Japanese: じゃあ、やっぱり‥‥ アイガ探偵なのかな‥‥。
English: So then... I guess it really is Detective Atmey, after all.

Japanese: この脅迫状‥‥やっぱり、 ブスジマ社長さんが？
English: So was this blackmail letter written by KB Security's CEO, Kane Bullard?

Japanese: たぶん、そうだと思います。
English: I-I think so... Probably.

Japanese: あの社長室、キーカードがないと 入れませんしね。
English: You can only get into the CEO's office if you have a key card.

Japanese: （脅迫状で、取り引きの場所が ＫＢ警備に指定されている‥‥）
English: (The blackmail letter said to meet at KB Security...)

Japanese: やっぱり、ブスジマさんしか 考えられないよね‥‥。
English: Then no one else other than Mr. Bullard could've have written the letter...

Japanese: ‥‥あの晩、午前１時‥‥ ボク、ＫＢ警備に行ったんです。
English: I was at KB Security at around 1 o'clock that night...

Japanese: サイフに入れておいたキーカードを 使って、ドアを開けたから‥‥
English: I took out my wallet to open the door with the key card...

Japanese: コイツは、そのとき 落としたワケですね？
English: So is that when you dropped it?

Japanese: スミマセン‥‥。今まで かくしていて。
English: I-I'm sorry... I should have told you the truth earlier.

Japanese: どうして、このカードを 優作くんが持っていたのかな？
English: What are you still doing with this key card anyway?

Japanese: ＫＢ警備をやめたときに、 会社に返さなかったの？
English: Didn't you return it when you left your job at KB Security?

Japanese: か、返しましたよ、もちろん！
English: Of course I returned it! Don't be silly!

Japanese: あの。このカードは、脅迫状と いっしょに送られてきたんです。
English: Umm... But it got sent back to me along with the blackmail letter.

Japanese: 脅迫状と‥‥？
English: With the blackmail letter...?

Japanese: “これを使って社長室に入れ” ‥‥って。
English: Yes, it said... "Use this to enter the CEO's office".

Japanese: いやー、ここまで人気者に なるとは思いませんでしたよ！
English: Oh! I didn't know Mask☆DeMasque had become so famous!

Japanese: このブロマイド、 あたしが買ったんですよ！
English: Yeah, I actually bought this publicity photo!

Japanese: あ。よかったら、サインしますよ！ 『怪人参上！』って。
English: Really? Well, I'd be happy to sign it if you like! I'll even add, "Behind Bars!"

Japanese: え！　ホントですか！
English: Yes! Please! Please!

Japanese: （優作くん‥‥すっかり 忘れてるみたいだな‥‥）
English: (It looks like Ron has totally forgotten about what happened at the trial today...)

Japanese: （今日の裁判で、無罪判決を もらったコト‥‥）
English: (That he was declared "not guilty" of being Mask☆DeMasque...)

Japanese: 社長室の非常ブザーですね。
English: That's the emergency buzzer from the CEO's office.

Japanese: 鳴らしちゃダメですよ。 ものスゴい音がするから。
English: Don't press it! It makes a terrible racket!

Japanese: へへ。あたし、思いっきり 鳴らしちゃいました。
English: Hee hee. Sorry. I just love making a lot of noise.

Japanese: ‥‥あの。そのブザー、 事件にカンケイあるんですか？
English: Err, umm... Does this buzzer have something to do with this case?

Japanese: 今のところ、なんとも 言えないですね。
English: Well, at this point, I don't really know yet.

Japanese: （ボタンに指紋は なかったわけだし‥‥）
English: (After all, there were no prints on it, or anything else for that matter.)

Japanese: ブスジマさんが殺害された時間‥‥ 一度だけ、鳴らされているんです。
English: The buzzer went off just once around the time that Bullard was killed.

Japanese: えええッ！　コワいですねー‥‥
English: Oooh! Th-That's scary!

Japanese: 優作くん、何か知らないかな？
English: Do you know anything about that, Mr. DeLite?

Japanese: ‥‥ううん。 特に思い当たるコトはないです。
English: ...Sorry, I'm afraid I don't.

Japanese: （‥‥たよりにならないな‥‥）
English: (Why am I not surprised by how clueless he is anymore...)

Japanese: このリスト‥‥ 社長室で見つけたんですけど。
English: This list... We found it in the office of the CEO, Mr. Bullard.

Japanese: これ、仮面マスクが盗んだ エモノのリストじゃないですか！
English: Hey! This is a list of all the things Mask☆DeMasque stole!

Japanese: 盗まれたモノの横に、 値段が書いてありますよね。
English: And that's the value of each item listed next to them.

Japanese: これって、秘宝のお値段‥‥ なんですか？
English: Are you sure that's the value of the stolen items?

Japanese: ううん‥‥それにしては ヤスい気がしますねー。
English: Hmm... The numbers seem a little low to me.

Japanese: 闇ルートで取り引きしても、 ３倍以上の値がつくと思いますよ。
English: Even on the black market, the prices should be at least three times higher than that.

Japanese: （さすが、元警備員。そのへんの 情報には詳しいみたいだな‥‥）
English: (I guess he knows about that stuff because he used to be a security guard...)

Japanese: 優作くん。この証言、 まちがいないね？
English: Mr. DeLite, are you absolutely sure about this testimony?

Japanese: 脅迫状で指示されたとおりに、 美術品を盗んだ‥‥
English: That you stole the treasures as you were directed by a series of blackmail letters...

Japanese: ええ。おカネももらえるし、 “これもアリかな”って。
English: Yes, and I got money for them in return. It seemed like a good deal.

Japanese: （ウソをついてるようには 見えないな‥‥）
English: (He doesn't look like he's lying...)

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥アイガ探偵の 告白は、なんだったのかな‥‥
English: So then how do you explain Detective Atmey's crazy confession...?

Japanese: あ、これ！　ボクのデビュー戦の 記事じゃないですか！
English: Hey! This is an article about my debut heist!

Japanese: いやー、タイヘンでしたよ。 いきなり追いつめられちゃって。
English: Boy, that was a tough one. Before I knew it, they were hot on my trail.

Japanese: でも‥‥仮面マスク、ちゃんと 逃げられたんですよね？
English: But Mask☆DeMasque... He must have gotten away, right?

Japanese: 記事にも《消えた》って 書いてあるし‥‥。
English: It says in the article that he "disappeared"...

Japanese: そうなんです！ とっさにヒラめいたんですよ！
English: That's right! I got a sudden burst of inspiration!

Japanese: そこにあるポリバケツの中に 怪人の衣装をかくして‥‥
English: I hid my Mask☆DeMasque costume in a nearby plastic bucket...

Japanese: 警備員に早変わりしたんです。 ‥‥ミゴトでしょう？
English: Then I quickly changed into my security guard uniform. ...Pretty clever, eh?

Japanese: へええ‥‥って、 ちょっと待ってください！
English: Wow... Awesome! ...Hey, hold the phone!

Japanese: この写真の警備員‥‥ 優作さん、なんですか！
English: The guard in this photo... Is this you, Mr. DeLite?

Japanese: そうですよ。 意外なトリックでしょう！
English: Hee hee. That's right. Nice trick if I do say so myself!

Japanese: （なんというか‥‥タンジュン すぎて、あきれちまうな）
English: (Nice and easy to figure out! Even Pearls could see through that in a heartbeat.)

Japanese: 証拠品《新聞記事》の データを書きなおした。
English: Newspaper Clipping updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、 さすがは名探偵ですよね。
English: But as you might expect, Detective Atmey found the disguise...

Japanese: ボクがバケツに捨てた怪人の衣装、 見つけちゃいましたから。
English: He truly deserves the title of "Ace Detective".

Japanese: アイガ探偵が‥‥怪人の衣装を？ （聞いたことなかったな‥‥）
English: Detective Atmey found the Mask☆DeMasque disguise? (Hmm, that's interesting...)

Japanese: ウチに持って帰っちゃった みたいですね、探偵さん。
English: Yes, and I heard that he brought it home with him.

Japanese: （哀牙探偵‥‥そのとき、 コイツを手にいれたワケか‥‥）
English: (So that's it... That's when Atmey got his hands on this.)

Japanese: おかげでボク、新しい衣装を 作り直したんですよ。
English: Thanks to that, I got the chance to remake my costume.

Japanese: それはタイヘンでしたねー‥‥
English: That must have been really time-consuming, huh?

Japanese: ‥‥その数日後でしたね。
English: Yes, it took quite a while to complete. Anyway, a few days after that...

Japanese: ボクのウチに、最初の脅迫状が 届いたのは‥‥。
English: I received the first of the blackmail letters...

Japanese: きょ‥‥キョーハクジョー！
English: B-Blackmail letters!?

Japanese: どど、どういうコトですか！ 優作くん！　話して！　ホラ早く！
English: Wh-What are you talking about? Hurry! Tell me what you mean!

Japanese: そそ、そんなに 身を乗り出さないでください‥‥
English: H-Hey, calm down...! Don't get so worked up...!

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 怪人☆仮面マスク　アジト
English: October 13 Mask☆DeMasque's Hideout

Japanese: あ！　リューイチくん！ マヨイちゃん！
English: Oh! Nicky boy! Maya!

Japanese: まれかさん‥‥
English: Ms. DeLite...

Japanese: アタシ‥‥ユーサクくんを 助けるつもりだったのに！
English: All I wanted to do was help my dear Ronnie...!

Japanese: ‥‥完全に逆効果でしたね。
English: Yeah... but I guess it ended up hurting his case...

Japanese: そういうコト言わない、 なるほどくん。
English: Don't say that, Nick! She doesn't need your help beating herself up.

Japanese: ねえ、リューイチくん。 助けてくれるでしょ？
English: Hey, Nicky boy! Please! Please help Ronnie!

Japanese: ユーサクくん‥‥殺人なんて、 ゼッタイ‥‥！
English: He's not a killer, I swear! My Ronnie wouldn't hurt a fly!

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、調べてみます。
English: ...Alright. I'll poke around and see what I can find out.

Japanese: ホントに！ ウレシイっ！
English: Really! Are you serious!? Oh, I'm so happy!

Japanese: ‥‥リューイチくんたちが いてくれて、よかったな。
English: I knew asking for your help was the right thing to do.

Japanese: アタシ‥‥もう、 どうしていいかわからなくて。
English: I... I don't know what I can do to help anymore...

Japanese: （‥‥意外にモロいところが あったんだな。まれかさん）
English: (I had no idea Ms. DeLite had such a vulnerable side.)

Japanese: これ‥‥仮面マスクだよ！
English: It's Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: ムネに、テープレコーダーが ブラさがっているな。
English: There's a tape recorder hanging from his neck.

Japanese: どれどれ。いじってみよう！
English: Let me see that!
...click...

Japanese: わははははははははははははは‥‥
English: Aha ha ha ha ha ha ha...

Japanese: ‥‥ゴクロウだったねえ、諸君。 宝冠はいただいて行くよ‥‥
English: I'll be taking this crown now! Better luck next time, gentlemen!

Japanese: わーははははははははははは‥‥
English: Aha ha ha ha ha ha ha...

Japanese: ユーサクくん、『ほがらかな笑い声 こそ、怪人のイノチだ』‥‥って。
English: Ronnie used to say that "a happy laugh is the lifeblood of a thief".

Japanese: たしかに、ココロが 洗われるような笑い声ですね。
English: Well, that certainly is a happy-sounding laugh alright.

Japanese: けっこう、笑うのって ムズカシイんだよ。
English: Yes, and laughing isn't as easy as it sounds.

Japanese: なんかこう、しらじらしく なっちゃってねー。
English: The harder you try, the more fake it sounds.

Japanese: うーん。優作さん、 ドリョク家なんだねえ‥‥。
English: Yeah. Mr. DeLite really works hard at his craft, huh?

Japanese: きゃあ、カワイイ！ これ、気球っていうんだよ！
English: Oh, that's so cute! It's called a hot air balloon, you know!

Japanese: ‥‥さすがに知ってるよ。
English: ...Wow! There's no fooling you!

Japanese: あたしも 作ってみようかなー、気球。
English: I want to make my own balloon now!

Japanese: なるほどくんも 乗せてあげるからね！
English: I'll even let you ride in it, Nick!

Japanese: （気球には、ゴクヒのはずの エンブレムがプリントされてる）
English: (That emblem on the balloon is supposed to be top-secret.)

Japanese: （‥‥天杉　優作が仮面マスクで あるという証拠だ）
English: (This could convince the courts that Ron DeLite is Mask☆DeMasque...)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんにはダマっておこう。 ‥‥コワいから）
English: (I'd better not tell Maya. She gets kinda... scary when it comes to "celebrities"...)

Japanese: 天杉　優作は‥‥脅迫状を 受け取っていたんだ。
English: So Ron received a blackmail letter, huh?

Japanese: まれかさんは知ってるのかな？ このコト‥‥
English: Do you think Ms. DeLite knows about this?

Japanese: 知ってたら、ニコニコ笑ってる ワケにも行かないだろ。
English: Well if she did, I don't think she would've been smiling like that.

Japanese: 教えてあげたほうが いいのかな‥‥
English: I wonder if we should tell her about it...

Japanese: よけいなコトは しないほうがいいと思うよ。
English: I think it'd be smarter if we didn't.

Japanese: 中途ハンパなヘンソウ道具が ガクに入ってかざられている。
English: It's a bunch of cheap costume pieces arranged inside a nice frame.

Japanese: つ、つけヒゲだよ なるほどくん！
English: L-Look, Nick! It's a fake beard!

Japanese: 厚紙を切りぬいた 仮面もあるぞ‥‥
English: There's also a mask made out of cardboard...

Japanese: ほらほら、なるほどくん。 つけヒゲつけヒゲ！
English: Here, Nick! Try on this fake beard! Come on! Try it on!

Japanese: う、うわ！ ナニするんだよ‥‥
English: H-Hey! Cut it out...

Japanese: ついでにマスクも かぶるかぶる！
English: Now put on the mask!

Japanese: あはははははははは！ 怪人なるほどくんだ！
English: Aha ha ha ha ha ha! It's Phoenix☆DeMasque!

Japanese: 気に入ったら 持っていっていいよ。
English: If you like it, feel free to take it home with you.

Japanese: いいです！
English: Thanks, but no thanks!

Japanese: （‥‥なんか、どこかで 見たことがあるような気がする）
English: (Hmmm... This thing looks vaguely familiar.)

Japanese: あ！　金魚ばちがあるよ。 逆さまだけど。
English: Oh, I know! It's a fishbowl! Well, an upside-down one, anyway...

Japanese: ヘルメットかもしれないよ。 ‥‥宇宙人の。
English: I think it's a helmet. ...An alien helmet.

Japanese: となりにあるのは、 テープレコーダーかな。
English: And that looks like a tape recorder or something next to it.

Japanese: 通信機かもしれないよ。 ‥‥宇宙人の。
English: I think it's a transceiver. ...An alien transceiver.

Japanese: あー、それ。優作くんが 持って帰ってきたんだよねー。
English: Oh, that. Ronnie brought that home one day.

Japanese: 会社のトモダチにムリ言って、 もらったんだって。
English: He asked one of his friends from work if he could have it.

Japanese: 宇宙レンジャーの制服。 優作くん、うれしそうだったなー。
English: It's a Space Ranger uniform. I've never seen Ronnie happier in his whole life.

Japanese: はあああ‥‥。 あたしもほしくなってきたよ。
English: Oh, man... Now I want one, too!

Japanese: （あの事件の警備会社‥‥ ＫＢ警備だったのか）
English: (I wonder if KB Security was in charge of security at the event from that old case?)

Japanese: ゴチャゴチャと いろいろ置いてある。
English: There's a whole bunch of stuff piled up over here.

Japanese: こっちの、ゴミ箱にささっている 巻きものはナニかな？
English: What are these rolls of paper stuck in the trash can?

Japanese: 《マザリンの宝石強奪 アルバトロス大作戦の巻》
English: "Theft of the Mazarin Stone: The Great Albatross Strategy"

Japanese: 《不死身の女神像強奪 ナックルボンバー大作戦の巻》
English: "Theft of the Immortal Goddess Statue: The Great Knucklebomber Strategy"

Japanese: （警察の人たち、ここは 捜査したのかな‥‥）
English: (Did the police even investigate this place...?)

Japanese: あー、警察のヒトなら、 見ていったよ、それ。
English: The police took a look at all of that, you know.

Japanese: え！　何か言ってましたか？
English: Really!? Did they say anything?

Japanese: “おハナシにならない”だって。
English: They said it wasn't important.

Japanese: そうですか‥‥。
English: Oh?

Japanese: しょうがないでしょ。ユーサクくん シロートなんだからさ。
English: Don't worry about it, Nicky boy. After all, my Ronnie is just an amateur.

Japanese: アタシは好きだけどな。 “ナックルボンバー”ってヒビキ。
English: Amateur or not, this is cool! "Knucklebomber"! How cool is that!?

Japanese: 本がドンとつんである。 かなりあるな。
English: There are a bunch of books lying here.

Japanese: ‥‥怪人やら怪盗やらが カツヤクする小説ばかりだ。
English: They're all romantic-sounding novels about famous heists and fantastic thieves.

Japanese: しおりやメモがいっぱい はさんであるよ。
English: Wow! Look at all the bookmarks and notes stuck into them!

Japanese: どれどれ。
English: Let's see here...

Japanese: 《注意：この怪人の笑いかたを 参考にすること》
English: "Note: Must remember this thief's way of laughing. Very useful."

Japanese: 《注意：やっぱり、予告状は 怪人としてのエチケット》
English: "Note: A calling card is an important part of any good thief's modus operandi!"

Japanese: いつか怪人になるのが ユメみたいだから。ユーサクくん。
English: It was always Ronnie's dream to become a thief.

Japanese: （‥‥もう、とっくに ユメをかなえてるのに‥‥）
English: (Well, I guess you can chalk that one down as fulfilled...)

Japanese: なんで優作さん、キチンと まれかさんに説明しないのかな。
English: I wonder why Mr. DeLite never talked to his wife about it?

Japanese: ‥‥気がつかない彼女にも 問題があると思うけど。
English: ...Yeah, well I think she deserves some of the blame for not bringing it up either.

Japanese: ぎょ‥‥魚雷があるよ、 なるほどくん！
English: Nick! It-It's a torpedo!

Japanese: ‥‥信じがたいけど、 ホンモノっぽいぞ、コレ。
English: ...It's hard to believe, but it sure looks real.

Japanese: まったく‥‥どこから 拾ってくるのかな、こんなの。
English: Where could he have picked up something like this?

Japanese: 落としモノみたいに言うなよ。
English: Please don't say that as if torpedoes are things people just leave lying around...

Japanese: アタシ、こういうヤツが イチバン、キライなんだよ。
English: I hate these kinds of people more than anything!

Japanese: あの‥‥メイタンテー、ですか？
English: Umm... You mean Ace Detectives?

Japanese: メイタンテーはユルせるよ。
English: No, I'm fine with Ace Detectives.

Japanese: ああ‥‥じゃあ、やっぱり カイジン‥‥だから？
English: Oh... So then you must mean thieves?

Japanese: モンダイなのは、メイタンテー のフリをしたカイジンであるコト。
English: No, they're alright, too. I just hate thieves that pretend to be Ace Detectives!

Japanese: アタシ、ヒキョーなオトコだけは ユルせないんだ！
English: There's nothing I hate more than cowardly men!

Japanese: ところで‥‥、どうして哀牙探偵の 事務所に行ったんですか？
English: By the way, why did you go to Detective Atmey's office anyway?

Japanese: いやー。いざ裁判の当日になると、 アタシも急に不安になっちゃって。
English: Well, as the trial went on, I started to get more and more nervous.

Japanese: 自分なりに、真犯人の情報を 集めよう！　って思ってさ。
English: I went there to try to find out more about the "real criminal".

Japanese: “しんはんにん”‥‥って‥‥
English: The "real criminal"...?

Japanese: 仮面マスクに決まってるでしょ。 ユーサクくんじゃないもん。
English: Yes! Obviously, the real Mask☆DeMasque is not my Ronnie, right?

Japanese: はあ‥‥
English: Y-Yeah...?

Japanese: 仮面マスクといえば、探偵の 星威岳ってヒトが詳しい、って‥‥
English: And Detective Atmey knew more about Mask☆DeMasque than anyone else...

Japanese: テレビの《街のスゴいヤツ》の コーナーで言ってたから。
English: They mentioned him on the "Great People of the Town" segment on TV.

Japanese: もしかして‥‥ハナシを 聞きに行ったんですか？
English: So then... you went there to ask him some questions?

Japanese: そうだよ。アタシだって、 ヤルときはヤルからね。
English: That's right. I'll do whatever it takes to save my man!

Japanese: 『センセイは、おルスですから！』 と泣きすがる秘書を振りきって、
English: His secretary said, "The Ace Detective isn't in right now!"

Japanese: 探偵さんのアジトに 押し入っちゃったワケ。
English: But I forced my way past her and into his hideout.

Japanese: （“あじと”ねえ‥‥）
English: (I wouldn't exactly call that office of his a "hideout"...)

Japanese: あのカバンなら、テーブルの上に ちょこん、って置いてあったよ。
English: That bag was sitting right there on top of the table.

Japanese: あ。きのうもそこに あったよね、あのカバン。
English: Oh yeah, we saw that bag there yesterday, too.

Japanese: ホント、ユルせないよ。 ヒトにツミを着せるなんて！
English: There's nothing lower than someone who would try to pin a crime on someone else.

Japanese: ね、聞いてよリューイチくん！
English: Listen carefully, Nicky boy!

Japanese: ユーサクくん、ぜーったい、 サツジンなんてできないんだから！
English: My Ronnie would never, ever kill anyone! It's just not in him!

Japanese: ‥‥それは、あたしもそう思うよ なるほどくん。
English: ...I don't think he would either, Nick.

Japanese: でもなあ。たまに優作くん、 カッとなるだろ。
English: Yeah, but you have to admit he's got a bit of a temper to him.

Japanese: 『死んじゃってくださーーーい！』 みたいなノリで‥‥
English: It's not that hard to imagine him just snapping and screaming, "Please diiiiie!"

Japanese: そういうコト言わない！
English: He would never say that!

Japanese: あの、まれかさん。 サツジンはともかく‥‥
English: Anyway, Ms. DeLite, he might not be a killer...

Japanese: 優作さん、まだ、自分のコト “怪人だ”って言ってますけど‥‥
English: ...but he's still going around saying that he's a "thief"...

Japanese: だからさ。それはユーサクくんの 思いこみであって‥‥
English: I already told you, that's just a fantasy for him...

Japanese: いやいや。思いこんでるのは まれかさんのほうじゃないですか？
English: Ms. DeLite, I hate to say it, but you're the one living in a fantasy world.

Japanese: なな、な‥‥ナニ言ってるの、 リューイチくん！
English: Wh-Wh-What!? How dare you say that to me, Nicky boy!

Japanese: アタシ、ユーサクくんのコトなら なんでも知ってるんだよ！
English: I know everything about my Ronnie! We don't have any secrets between us!

Japanese: 石橋を叩いてもわたらない‥‥ っていうか、
English: Ronnie isn't the thief type! He's so honest that he wouldn't even sneak a nap!

Japanese: コワれるまで叩きつづけるほど 弱気な性格なのに‥‥怪人なんて！
English: He-He's so honest that he wouldn't even steal a glance! Thief! Ha! The very idea...!

Japanese: （そもそも‥‥ フシギだよなあ‥‥）
English: Hmm... I guess I just don't get it...

Japanese: え。何が？
English: Huh? Get what?

Japanese: こんなにタイプのちがう２人が、 どうして夫婦になったんだろ。
English: I just can't understand how they can be so different, and yet, be such a happy couple!

Japanese: それはまあ、たしかにねえ‥‥。
English: Yeah, they sure are different.

Japanese: やだなー、リューイチくん。 それはホラ。しょうがないでしょ？
English: Come on now, Nicky boy! It's not that mysterious, is it?

Japanese: もお、ホレちゃったんだもん。 ‥‥アタシが。
English: It was love at first sight! ...For me, anyway.

Japanese: え！ まま、まれかさんが‥‥？
English: What!? F-For you?

Japanese: ‥‥あの。ホレちゃったんですか？ まれかさんが？　優作さんに？
English: Umm... So was it really love at first sight when you first met Mr. DeLite?

Japanese: ‥‥ユーサクくんはね。 イノチの恩人なんだよ。
English: Well, maybe not at first sight, but Ronnie saved my life.

Japanese: イノチの‥‥？
English: Saved your life...?

Japanese: ムカシ、つとめてた会社にね。 ２人組の強盗が入ったんだ。
English: I was at work one day when two robbers suddenly rushed in.

Japanese: アタシ、そういうのユルせなくて。 ダンコ、抗議してやったの。
English: Well, I'm not the kind to just curl into a little ball in a corner, so I fought back.

Japanese: ごご、強盗に、ですか？
English: R-Robbers?

Japanese: そしたら、人質にとられちゃって。 コワかったんだよ、ホントに。
English: Yes, they took me hostage. I was so frightened.

Japanese: ２人とも、大きなナイフ持ってて。 泣いちゃったもん、アタシ。
English: They were both carrying these huge knives and... I-I broke down into tears.

Japanese: （そりゃそうだろう。 ぼくですら、まちがいなく泣く）
English: (Yeah... I would too, if I were in that situation.)

Japanese: あ！　も、もしかして‥‥ それを助けてくれたのが‥‥？
English: Oh! I think I get it! Did Mr. DeLite come running to save you?

Japanese: そう！　ユーサクくんだったの！ ガードマンの制服でキメてたよ。
English: Yes! Exactly! I remember he looked so handsome in that guard uniform of his!

Japanese: ナイフを持ったヤツらに向かって、 まず、ガツンとドナリつけたんだ。
English: He went right up to those two knife-wielding robbers... and screamed in their faces!

Japanese: “やめてくださーーーい！”って。 強盗たちも、ヒルんじゃって。
English: "PLEASE, STOP IIIIIIIIIIT!" he screamed. I could see the robbers' faces turn pale...

Japanese: （あの悲痛なウラ声は、ミョーに ココロにつき刺さるからな‥‥）
English: (That high-pitched shriek of his does have a surprisingly strong effect on people...)

Japanese: そして、泣きながら両手ふり回して 強盗につかみかかっていったワケ。
English: Then, crying and swinging his arms like crazy, he attacked the two robbers.

Japanese: ‥‥見ず知らずの、このアタシを 助けるために。たったヒトリで。
English: All by himself... He came to save me, a total stranger, all by himself...

Japanese: 自分が刺されるかもしれないのに。 ガクガクふるえながら。
English: He was so scared that he was crying and shaking, but he still risked his life for me.

Japanese: ‥‥はああ。 いいおハナシですねえ‥‥。
English: ...Wow. That's a great story...

Japanese: ああ、この人はイザというとき、 イノチをかけて戦えるヒトだ‥‥
English: Yes. He may not look it, but in a tough situation, there's no one better...

Japanese: ‥‥アタシね、そう思ったんだ。 うれしかったよ。
English: ...That's why I fell in love with him like I did.

Japanese: それは‥‥ホレちゃうかも しれませんねー。
English: *sniff sniff* Th-That's so romantic... I'd fall in love too, I guess.

Japanese: なるほどくん、シッカリたのむよ。 あたしが人質にとられたときは。
English: Nick, I hope you'll do the same for me if I ever get taken hostage...

Japanese: （この子の場合、そういうコトが ホントにありそうで、コワい）
English: (With Maya, that possibility always seems to loom in the not so distant future. *gulp*)

Japanese: まれかさん。《ＫＢ警備》って ご存じですか？
English: Ms. DeLite, do you know about KB Security?

Japanese: もちろん。ユーサクくんが つとめている会社だもん。
English: Don't be silly, of course I do. That's where my Ronnie works.

Japanese: （“つとめている”会社‥‥か）
English: (So she thinks he still works there, huh...)

Japanese: （たしか、ぼくたちが今日、 聞いたハナシでは‥‥）
English: (Oh, that's right. According to what we heard today...)

Japanese: まさか‥‥ 知らねえとは言わせねえぜ。
English: Come on! Don't tell me you didn't know it!

Japanese: 天杉　優作はムカシ、 ＫＢ警備会社の社員‥‥
English: Ron DeLite was once an employee of KB Security...

Japanese: プロの警備員だったんだ。
English: He was a professional security guard.

Japanese: （‥‥今はもう、やめているはず なんだよな‥‥あの会社）
English: (...But he quit. He doesn't work there anymore.)

Japanese: まれかさん‥‥ 知らないみたいだね。
English: It looks like Ms. DeLite doesn't know...

Japanese: ＫＢ警備なら、ウチから２０分 ぐらいかな。バイクで飛ばして。
English: KB Security is only about 20 minutes away. By motorcycle, that is.

Japanese: 矢張はクルマで３０分、って 言ってましたけど‥‥
English: Larry told me it takes 30 minutes by car...

Japanese: なんてったって、 飛ばすからさ。あたしのバイク。
English: Well, I have to admit I tend to fly pretty fast on my bike.

Japanese: （“飛ばす”って‥‥ 空でも飛ぶのか？）
English: (To make it to KB Security that fast, are you sure you aren't literally flying...?)

Japanese: ‥‥あ。なんだったら、 乗せていってあげようか。今から。
English: Why don't I give you a ride sometime? Or better yet, how about now?

Japanese: い、いえいえいえ‥‥。そんな！
English: Um, ahh... N-No...! I'll pass, th-thanks!

Japanese: とりあえず、場所だけ 教えてもらえればケッコウです。
English: Wh-Why don't you just tell us where it is and we'll go ourselves.

Japanese: あーあ。いくじなしだなー、 なるほどくんは。
English: Ha ha, what a scaredy-cat you are, Nick!

Japanese: （まれかさんからＫＢ警備の 場所を教えてもらった）
English: (Ms. DeLite told us the location of KB Security.)

Japanese: よーし。じゃあサッソク 行ってみようか、なるほどくん！
English: OK. Let's head over there right away, Nick!

Japanese: 《倉院のツボ》かあ。 よかったね、戻ってきて。
English: I'm so happy that you got your Sacred Urn back.

Japanese: まれかさんのおかげです！ ‥‥ごめんなさい。
English: Well, I have you to thank for that, Ms. DeLite! ...A-And I'm sorry.

Japanese: ‥‥？　なんで、あやまるの？
English: Huh...? What are you apologizing for?

Japanese: だって‥‥ツボを盗んだハンニンは 優作さんだ、って思ったから‥‥
English: Because I mistakenly blamed your husband for taking it...

Japanese: あはは。だから言ったでしょ。
English: Ha! I told you, didn't I?

Japanese: ユーサクくんが 怪人のワケ、ないって。
English: My Ronnie could never do such a thing.

Japanese: あの、これなんですけど‥‥
English: Umm, so what do you know about this...?

Japanese: だ、ダメだよなるほどくん！
English: No! You can't show her that, Nick!

Japanese: そのエンブレム、ゴクヒだって 言われたでしょ！
English: That emblem is top-secret! Don't you remember!?

Japanese: あ‥‥そうだったっけ。
English: ...Doh! Oops, sorry about that.

Japanese: （ゴクヒと言われると、よけい 見せびらかしたくなるよな‥‥）
English: (But the way she just said "top-secret" was a worse giveaway than what I did...)

Japanese: あたしも、ゴクヒって言われると よけい見たくなるよー。
English: Ooh! When I hear the word "top-secret", it just piques my interest like nothing else!

Japanese: あの‥‥これ、なんですけど。 知ってますか？
English: Umm... Do you know about this?

Japanese: ああ、ユーサクくん宛ての お手紙ね？
English: Oh, is that a letter addressed to my Ronnie?

Japanese: アタシ、ヒトのものを勝手に 盗み見るの、キライだから。
English: Sorry, but I hate it when people poke their noses into other people's business.

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり、まれかさんは 知らないみたいだね。
English: It looks like Ms. DeLite doesn't know about it...

Japanese: 優作さんが キョーハクされていたコト‥‥
English: Which means she has no idea that her husband was being blackmailed...

Japanese: ユーサクくんのサイフだね、それ。
English: That's Ronnie's wallet.

Japanese: 事件があった夜、ＫＢ警備の ビルで見つかったんですけど‥‥
English: It was found at KB Security on the night of the murder...

Japanese: あの晩は、ユーサクくんと レストランでゴハン食べて‥‥
English: Ronnie and I went out to dinner early that evening...

Japanese: そのときは、たしかに あったんだけどなー、それ。
English: I'm sure he had his wallet with him then.

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥優作くんが 現場で落としたのかな‥‥）
English: (So it's true then. He must have dropped it at the crime scene...)

Japanese: あ！　知ってるよ、それ。
English: Ah! I know what that is!

Japanese: ユーサクくんの会社の カードだから。
English: That's one of those key cards from Ronnie's company.

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば、ひさしぶりに 見た気がするなあ‥‥。
English: ...Come to think of it, it's been a long time since I've seen one.

Japanese: （会社はやめていたみたい だからな‥‥優作くん）
English: (That's probably because Ron doesn't work there anymore...)

Japanese: これが怪人☆仮面マスクかー。 どんなウチに住んでるのかなあ。
English: So this is Mask☆DeMasque, huh? I wonder what kind of a house he lives in.

Japanese: こんなウチ‥‥かもしれませんね。
English: Who knows... Maybe it's exactly like this house.

Japanese: あはははは。 それ、おもしろいねー。
English: Ha ha ha, you're so funny sometimes, Nicky boy.

Japanese: でも、アタシが怪人なら、もっと ゴージャスなお屋敷に住みたいな。
English: But if I was a famous thief, I'd live in a great big, gorgeous mansion.

Japanese: ‥‥優作くんに伝えておきます。
English: ...I'll be sure to tell that to your husband.

Japanese: すみません。 これ、なんですけど‥‥
English: Excuse me, but would you take a look at this...?

Japanese: やー、ゴメン。 アタシ、意外に世間知らずでさー。
English: Oh, I'm sorry... I haven't the faintest idea.

Japanese: ちょっと、わからないな。 そういうの。
English: I guess I just don't get out enough.

Japanese: リューイチくん、 弁護士なんだよね。
English: So, Nicky boy, you're a lawyer, huh?

Japanese: ええ、まあ。
English: Well, yeah.

Japanese: どう？　弁護士って。 スリルがあるんじゃない？
English: How do you like it? I bet it's SO exciting!

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、たしかに。 不必要なほどスリリングですね。
English: ...Yeah, sometimes. Actually, a little too exciting at times.

Japanese: ‥‥アタシ、大好物 なんだよねー、スリル。
English: Oooh! I just love a good thrill.

Japanese: ‥‥ふうん‥‥いいなあ、 リューイチくんは‥‥
English: Hmm... Nicky boy... It sounds like you have it so nice...

Japanese: （‥‥うう‥‥ いごこち悪いな、どうも）
English: (Um... This is starting to get awkward.)

Japanese: たいしたもんだよねー、 その若さで家元なんてさ。
English: That's really amazing! A "Master" at your age!

Japanese: い、いえいえ。今の家元は、 あたしのおかあさんなんですけど。
English: Oh no, you've got it wrong. It's my mother that's the current Master!

Japanese: あ。そうなんだ。 ‥‥ところでさ。なんの家元？
English: Oh, I see... So what exactly is she the master of anyway?

Japanese: えーと、れいば
English: Well, you see, it's li--

Japanese: やめとけよ。“霊媒”なんて 言っても、笑われるだけだぞ。
English: Don't say it, Maya. If you say "spirit channeling", she'll laugh.

Japanese: ううう‥‥さみしいなあ。
English: Ouch... That hurt.

Japanese: あ、この子。 リューイチくんにビンタしてた。
English: Oh, this girl. I saw her slap Nicky boy right across the face.

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥ （そのおぼえ方はやめてほしいな）
English: Um, ah, yeah, she did... (Of all the ways she could remember Pearls by... Why?)

Japanese: かわいいよねー。 今度さ。あたしにも紹介してよ。
English: She's such a little cutie. If you could bring her by, I'd love to meet her.

Japanese: ええ！　いいですよ。
English: Sure! Of course!

Japanese: （‥‥また、ひっぱたかれそうな 気がする‥‥）
English: (Great... I can just tell I'm in for a reprise of the "Hundred Hand Slap"...)

Japanese: たよりなさそうに見えるけどねー。
English: I know he doesn't look it...

Japanese: これでも、やるときは ビシッとやるんだよ。
English: But he can really get things done when he puts his mind to it.

Japanese: へえ！　いつなんですか？ その“やるとき”って。
English: Really? So when exactly does he put his mind to it?

Japanese: ‥‥まあ。 めったにないけど。
English: ...Well. Not very often, I admit.

Japanese: ‥‥でもね。アタシは 一生、忘れないと思うな。
English: ...But I'll never forget the first time we met as long as I live.

Japanese: ステキな思い出が あるんだね、まれかさん。
English: It must be nice to have such a fond memory like that...

Japanese: ゼッタイ、ユルせないよ、 こんなヤツ。
English: I'll never forgive this man.

Japanese: 自分のツミを、ユーサクくんに 着せようなんて。
English: He tried to pin his own crimes on my Ronnie.

Japanese: そうですよね！
English: I don't blame you!

Japanese: 犯罪者って、みんなそう！ 自分勝手で‥‥ヒキョウで！
English: All criminals are the same! They're nothing but filthy, sneaking liars!

Japanese: アタシ、大っキライ！
English: I hate their guts!

Japanese: （‥‥なんとなく、 わかる気がするな‥‥）
English: (*gulp* OK, I think I get it...)

Japanese: （ユーサクくんが、怪人のコトを まれかさんに話してない理由）
English: (...And I think I can see why Ron didn't tell her how he was earning that money.)

Japanese: アタシは、バイクとユーサクくんが いれば、シアワセだなー。
English: As long as I've got my motorcycle and my Ronnie, I'm happy.

Japanese: うわあ、ステキですね！
English: Oh! That's so sweet!

Japanese: あとは‥‥少しのスリルと、 少しじゃないお金があれば。
English: Oh, that plus a little excitement and a little shopping money.

Japanese: それで、もうじゅうぶんかな。
English: ...THEN I'm happy.

Japanese: ‥‥意外によくばりだよ、 なるほどくん‥‥
English: Now she's starting to sound a bit greedy... Don't you think, Nick?

Japanese: あー、サイフを 届けてくれた、カレだね。
English: Oh, this is the guy who delivered Ronnie's wallet.

Japanese: 『やっぱり』が口グセの。
English: He's got some "issues", doesn't he?
...Huh?

Japanese: しゃべってたら、不自然なほど 『やっぱり』を連呼するから、
English: Let's just say his flirting style is pretty obvious. ...Pretty stale too, actually.

Japanese: 名前は忘れちゃったけど、 そのクセだけ、印象に残ってるな。
English: It's pretty sad when the only thing you remember about a man are his lame pick-up lines.

Japanese: （たぶん、自分を売りこもうと 必死だったんだろうな‥‥矢張）
English: (What's even more sad is said man probably believes in those like they're his life-lines.)

Japanese: ‥‥作戦はシッパイだった みたいだけどね。
English: ...Looks like he blew it again, huh?

Japanese: こいつ‥‥シャチョーだね、 ユーサクくんの会社の。
English: Oh, I know him... This is Ronnie's boss, the CEO guy.

Japanese: 亡くなったんですよ。 ゆうべ、ナニモノかに襲われて。
English: Well, now he's an ex-boss. Someone killed him last night.

Japanese: ‥‥うん。モチロン知ってるよ。
English: Naturally, I know that.

Japanese: なんかね。ズルがしこい ヤツだったな。
English: He was a pretty sly man, let me tell you.

Japanese: そうなんですか‥‥
English: Oh really...?

Japanese: ユーサクくんに《警備チームの チーフ》っていう肩書きをつけて、
English: You bet he was. He made Ronnie "Chief" of one of the security teams.

Japanese: さんざん、コキ使われてた からね。もう、スリ切れるほど。
English: But that was just so he could work him like a slave.

Japanese: ‥‥優作さん、 社長さんにモンクは‥‥？
English: Did Mr. DeLite ever have a problem with Mr. Bullard himself?

Japanese: （言えるワケ、ないよな‥‥） it...?)
English: (Even if he did, you think she'd ever tell you about

Japanese: ユーサクくん、そういうコト、 あんまりギモンに思わないし。
English: Well Ronnie isn't the type to complain about that kind of thing.

Japanese: アタシががんばっても、 相手にされなくってさ‥‥
English: I tried to get him to talk to me about work, but he never would.

Japanese: イヤなヤツだよね、ホント。
English: Grrr, I don't like this man one bit.

Japanese: 最初っから、狙ってたんだ。 ユーサクくんのコト。
English: He's had it in for my Ronnie from the very beginning.

Japanese: ‥‥サツジンハンとして、 疑いをかけてたんだよ！
English: It's his fault that Ronnie is under suspicion of committing murder.

Japanese: ぼくは、コーヒーを かけられましたしね。
English: Plus, he lobbed a coffee grenade at me!

Japanese: かけられっぱなしじゃ カッコわるいよね‥‥
English: You looked pretty silly wearing that mug on your head like a birthday hat...

Japanese: よし。明日、アイツに 牛乳ひっかけちゃおうよ！
English: Oh, I know! We can get him back tomorrow by returning fire with a milk grenade!

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 哀牙探偵事務所
English: October 13 Atmey Detective Agency

Japanese: ‥‥ものすごい数の警官が、 ものすごいイキオイで捜査中だ。
English: Whoa, this place is literally crawling with cops!

Japanese: そりゃまあ‥‥仮面マスクの正体が じつは名探偵だった、なんて‥‥
English: What did you expect? Now that they know he was actually Mask☆DeMasque...

Japanese: 警察にしてみれば、これ以上 マヌケなコトはないもんねー。
English: This must be incredibly embarrassing for them, don't you think?

Japanese: メンツにかけて 捜査をしてる、ってワケか。
English: Yeah, I guess they're trying to make up for it by tearing the place apart.

Japanese: そういえば‥‥ イトノコ刑事が、いないぞ。
English: Hey, I just noticed... Gumshoe is nowhere to be seen.

Japanese: 殺人事件の捜査じゃない？
English: Well, he is a homicide detective. He's probably working on the murder case.

Japanese: でも、きのうまで仮面マスクの 担当だったのに‥‥
English: But wasn't he in charge of the Mask☆DeMasque investigation all the way up to yesterday?

Japanese: やっぱり、シゲキ的だからね。 サツジンのほうが。
English: Well, a murder case is a lot more exciting, isn't it?

Japanese: “そっちのほうが楽しそうッスー” ‥‥なーんて。
English: He'd say something like: "There's nothin' like a good murder case, pal."

Japanese: （‥‥浮気な刑事さんだな‥‥）
English: (Points for the quality of the impression, but I'm not sure Gumshoe has bloodlust, Maya.)

Japanese: コラッ！　キミタチッ！
English: Hey! You guys!

Japanese: きゃっ！
English: Eeek!

Japanese: 勝手にさわっちゃイカン！ ワレワレが捜査中だッ！
English: We're investigating this place! So keep your hands to yourselves!

Japanese: なるほどくん。 フキゲンだよ‥‥警察のヒトたち。
English: Boy! These policemen sure are a bunch of sourpusses!

Japanese: そりゃ、名探偵にコケにされて ゴキゲンってわけにもいかないよ。
English: Well, Mr. Ace Detective did make complete and utter fools out of them...

Japanese: さあさあ、帰りなさいッ！ ほらほら、今すぐッ！
English: What's all this now! Move it along! The show's over!

Japanese: ううう‥‥。調べさせて くれそうにないよ。
English: Aww... It doesn't look like they're going to let us investigate.

Japanese: （今日は、あきらめたほうが いいみたいだな‥‥）
English: (I guess we might as well call it a day for this place and go look somewhere else.)
